executive_conclusion: Integrity is strong: the loader compiles, both test suites pass (46/46 weekly; 20 pass + 1 known skip readonly), the default is no-write, the apply guards for week/hash/confirm-token/db_url/postcheck are strong, there is no shell=True, no free-SQL CLI, the DSN is redacted, and sql/11 carries the NO APPLY header. However, the DDL-plus-loader unit cannot yet perform a correct, reproducible, reversible weekly replacement. Three blockers sit at or before the DDL boundary: (1) the resolved view re-admits legacy-inferred batches for an explicitly-assigned week, so deleted grains (the 70 bajas) survive and the replacement policy is silently defeated; (2) the loader's required resolved-view column and its postcheck reference cod_rt_norm, which the sql/11 view does not expose (it exposes cod_rt), so verify_db_contract aborts apply and the duplicate-grain postcheck references a non-existent column; (3) the assignment lifecycle never supersedes the prior ACTIVE row and hardcodes replaces_ruta_batch_id=None, so a second (retroactive) replacement of the same week violates the unique ACTIVE index. Rollback restoration is an unimplemented stub, the postcheck over-claims relative to POSTCHECK_CONTRACT, the written resolved_surface_hash is not comparable to any DB-computed hash, and the multirow tiebreak is source_row/order dependent while BD-CG005B-001 is still open. The earliest failing gate is the DDL itself (the resolved view semantics and the loader/view column contract), so the verdict is BLOCKED_BEFORE_DDL; apply is also separately blocked.

scope_and_method.method: Static reading of loader + SQL contract + tests + consumers; executed py_compile and the two named unittest suites against the working tree (identical to 074ee5b for these files); an isolated in-memory python simulation of the resolved-view ORDER BY (no productive file modified).

validation_a_integrity.sql_no_apply_evidence: sql/11 line 1 '-- NO APPLY IN CG005C'; file wrapped begin; ... rollback;

readonly_tests.skip_reason: Windows symlink privilege unavailable (pre-existing extractor test)

validation_a_integrity.default_no_write_evidence: validate_cli_args: if not args.apply -> args.dry_run=True; main() emits plan and returns for dry_run; test_01 asserts connect_db not called.
validation_a_integrity.no_free_sql_evidence: No subprocess; all SQL is module-level parameterized constants; DELETE is 'DELETE FROM public.ruta_rutero WHERE source = %s'; CLI has no --sql/--stdin (test_31).
validation_a_integrity.dsn_evidence: redact_secret() on error strings; dsn_printed=false in plan/result/error payloads; test_28/test_29.

validation_b_replacement_semantics.point: explicit batch used exclusively as authoritative snapshot
validation_b_replacement_semantics.fact: The resolver prefers EXPLICIT_ASSIGNMENT per grain, but the resolved view joins v_ruta_rutero_load_batch_week_v2 (union of explicit + legacy), so the explicit batch is not exclusive for the week.
validation_b_replacement_semantics.evidence: sql/11:200-202 join to v_ruta_rutero_load_batch_week_v2; 92-126 union all explicit_assignment + legacy_inferred.

validation_b_replacement_semantics.point: deleted grain cannot reappear from a legacy batch of the same week
validation_b_replacement_semantics.fact: legacy_inferred excludes a batch only if THAT batch has an ACTIVE assignment, not if the week is already explicitly assigned; a legacy batch whose loaded_at infers to the assigned week still contributes rows, so a grain absent from the explicit batch survives from the legacy batch.
validation_b_replacement_semantics.evidence: sql/11:104-120 legacy_inferred NOT EXISTS keyed on a.ruta_batch_id=b.ruta_batch_id only.

validation_b_replacement_semantics.point: the 70 deleted grains cease to exist in the resolved surface
validation_b_replacement_semantics.fact: Because legacy backfill is not prevented and the postcheck does not compare resolved grains to the new batch, bajas dropped in the new file persist via the legacy-inferred batch.
validation_b_replacement_semantics.evidence: Resolved view reads cg_core.ruta_rutero_load_rows (immutable history), not public.ruta_rutero; legacy batch rows remain; postcheck (run_postcheck) has no stale/baja check.

validation_b_replacement_semantics.point: historical batches available for audit but do not silently fill snapshot gaps
validation_b_replacement_semantics.fact: Historical batches both remain for audit AND silently fill gaps in the explicitly-assigned week through the resolved view union.
validation_b_replacement_semantics.evidence: sql/11:121-126 union all; loader REQUIRED_DB_OBJECTS keeps history; no exclusivity filter.

validation_b_replacement_semantics.point: resolved view produces exactly one grain effective_week_start + COD_RT + CLIENTE
validation_b_replacement_semantics.fact: logical_rank partitions by (effective_week_start, cod_rt, cliente_norm) and filters logical_rank=1, yielding one row per grain.
validation_b_replacement_semantics.caveat: The grain is on cliente_norm (normalized), and the loader/postcheck duplicate-grain check references cod_rt_norm which the view does not expose (see F-02).

validation_c_assignment_lifecycle.scenario: Week 2026-06-08: assignment A ACTIVE; correction creates batch B; B should replace A; A should become SUPERSEDED; B ACTIVE; rollback should reactivate A.
validation_c_assignment_lifecycle.unique_active_index_evidence: sql/11:80-82 ux_ruta_rutero_week_assignment_active on (effective_week_start, route_policy_version) where assignment_status='ACTIVE'.
validation_c_assignment_lifecycle.locking_evidence: run_weekly_replacement_apply takes no advisory lock and no SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on the week's assignments.
validation_c_assignment_lifecycle.order_of_operations: register batch -> insert history -> validate count -> snapshot previous -> delete public -> insert public -> validate count -> create ACTIVE assignment -> postcheck -> finalize ok -> commit.
validation_c_assignment_lifecycle.replaces_evidence: run_weekly_replacement_apply calls create_week_assignment(..., replaces_ruta_batch_id=None) at loader line 1110.
validation_c_assignment_lifecycle.supersede_evidence: No UPDATE sets a prior ACTIVE assignment to SUPERSEDED anywhere; create_week_assignment only INSERTs status 'ACTIVE'.
validation_c_assignment_lifecycle.second_replacement_evidence: A second ACTIVE insert for the same (week, policy) violates ux_ruta_rutero_week_assignment_active -> IntegrityError -> except -> conn.rollback().
validation_c_assignment_lifecycle.concurrency: Unprotected; relies on row locks + unique index; risk of deadlock or opaque failure (fails closed, no corruption).
validation_c_assignment_lifecycle.conclusion: The first-ever load to a fresh week succeeds, but the named retroactive-replacement cycle (B supersedes A; rollback reactivates A) is not implemented and a same-week re-apply is rejected by the unique ACTIVE index.

validation_d_multirow_precedence.rule: Resolved logical_rank ORDER BY: EXPLICIT_ASSIGNMENT first, visitas_exigidas desc, (lunes+...+domingo) desc, source_row asc, row_hash asc (sql/11:211-220).
validation_d_multirow_precedence.business_approval_evidence: BD-CG005B-001 (non-exact multirow collapse) is still open in research/CG005B_ROUTE_REPLACEMENT_CONTRACT.json; the resolver already collapses to logical_rank=1, pre-empting the decision.

fixtures.scenario: same rows, inverted order (exact duplicate row_hash)
fixtures.reason: exact_dup_rank by (ruta_batch_id, row_hash) collapses to one row with identical content.

fixtures.scenario: same frequency and day-sum, different persons
fixtures.reason: visitas and day-sum tie -> tiebreak source_row asc -> winner flips when Excel order/source_row changes (proven: order1=REPO_A, order2=REPO_B).
fixtures.method: isolated python simulation of the ORDER BY; no productive file modified

fixtures.scenario: different modality and responsible, different frequency
fixtures.reason: frequency (visitas) differs -> business field decides deterministically (proven: REPO_C both orders).

validation_d_multirow_precedence.frequency_days_modality_responsibles_pre: Per selected logical row only; the unselected operational row is dropped from the resolved surface (preserved only in history).
validation_d_multirow_precedence.stable_between_excel_reorders: Only when a business field (visitas or day-sum) differs; unstable when persons differ but schedule ties.
validation_d_multirow_precedence.conclusion: Precedence is deterministic given fixed source_row, but it materially depends on source_row for the person-only-differs class, contradicting the contract statement that source_row must not be material, and it collapses multirow groups before BD-CG005B-001 is decided.

inventory.name: workbook_sha256
inventory.algorithm: SHA-256 of full file bytes, uppercase

inventory.name: schema_signature
inventory.algorithm: SHA-256 of normalized header keys joined by '|', uppercase
inventory.comparable: not compared to any stored expected signature

inventory.name: row_hash
inventory.algorithm: MD5 of BUSINESS_HASH_COLUMNS values joined by '|'
inventory.purpose: per-row business fingerprint; exact-dup detection; resolver tiebreak
inventory.consumer: history, public, exact dedup, resolved view

inventory.name: python_current_surface_hash
inventory.algorithm: SHA-256 of 'source_row|row_hash' lines joined by newline, uppercase

inventory.name: db_current_surface_hash
inventory.comparable: NOT comparable to python_current_surface_hash (md5 vs sha256; different column tuple)

inventory.name: assignment_resolved_surface_hash
inventory.columns: source_row, row_hash of current public surface
inventory.purpose: claims to represent the resolved surface

validation_e_hashes.comparability_fact: The hash written to the assignment is Python SHA-256 over source_row|row_hash of the CURRENT public surface; the only DB-side hash (fetch_current_snapshot_summary) is MD5 over row_hash alone. Different algorithm and different columns; no equality check exists. The name 'resolved_surface_hash' is misleading: it does not derive from the resolved weekly view (which collapses to logical grain).

broken: resolved_duplicate_logical_grains_zero references cod_rt_norm which the sql/11 resolved view does not expose -> would raise UndefinedColumn at apply time

validation_f_transaction.psycopg2_transaction_start: conn.autocommit=False then implicit transaction begins on first execute (verify_db_contract); explicit cur.execute('BEGIN') is issued afterwards (redundant, emits a nested-BEGIN warning).
validation_f_transaction.queries_before_begin: verify_db_contract runs SELECTs after autocommit=False but before the explicit BEGIN; they are already inside the implicit transaction, so atomicity is unaffected.
validation_f_transaction.locks_needed: none acquired (no advisory lock / SELECT FOR UPDATE)
validation_f_transaction.delete_evidence: PUBLIC_DELETE_SQL = 'DELETE FROM public.ruta_rutero WHERE source = %s' (loader; test_18/test_19).
validation_f_transaction.insert: execute_values into public.ruta_rutero (no ON CONFLICT; test_20).
validation_f_transaction.batch_finalization: finalize_cg_ruta_batch status 'ok' only after postcheck (test_23 asserts finalize not called on postcheck failure).
validation_f_transaction.race_behavior: Two operators on the same week serialize via row locks on the public source slice and the unique ACTIVE index; the loser errors out (no advisory lock, possible deadlock). Fails closed (no partial commit).
validation_f_transaction.fail_after_delete: Same-transaction rollback restores the deleted public rows (no commit yet).
validation_f_transaction.fail_after_assignment: Same-transaction rollback discards the assignment and all prior writes.

validation_g_rollback.argument_validation_evidence: run_weekly_replacement_rollback validates week (Monday), db_url, failed_assignment_id, expected_current_surface_hash, confirm_token==ROUTE_WEEK_ROLLBACK_V1.
validation_g_rollback.sql_design_evidence: sql/11:261-279 commented rollback transaction design (lock, validate hash, mark ROLLED_BACK, reactivate previous, delete+restore public, postvalidate).
validation_g_rollback.restoration_evidence: After verify_db_contract, run_weekly_replacement_rollback unconditionally raises MissingDBContractError(['rollback_execution_requires_separate_authorization']) and rolls back; it never restores public.ruta_rutero, never reactivates the previous assignment, never marks the failed assignment ROLLED_BACK.
validation_g_rollback.restoration_test_evidence: test_40 only asserts callable(run_weekly_replacement_rollback) and the confirm-token constant.
validation_g_rollback.authorization: Self-blocks with an explicit 'requires separate authorization' error.
validation_g_rollback.conclusion: Rollback is interface + argument-validation + commented design only; postcommit restoration is not implemented and not tested.

validation_h_cli_bypass.fact: --skip-source-check is not rejected with --apply; a skipped source-check returns final_verdict 'warn' (not block), so apply proceeds. Missing critical columns would still fail later inside transform_route_dataframe (fails closed), but the explicit guard is bypassable.
validation_h_cli_bypass.evidence: build_arg_parser --skip-source-check; source_check_payload_skipped final_verdict 'warn'; validate_cli_args does not forbid skip+apply.

validation_h_cli_bypass.fact: Week is required and Monday-validated, but not cross-checked against the workbook content or the contracted 2026-06-08..2026-06-14; any Monday is accepted.
--apply requires --expected-workbook-sha256 and build_dry_run_plan raises workbook_hash_mismatch on mismatch.

verify_db_contract checks relations, assignment/view columns, batch status constraint, and read_only; but it requires resolved-view column cod_rt_norm that sql/11 does not provide, so it fails against the companion DDL (see F-02).
Legacy flags are argparse.SUPPRESS no-ops; the old write path was removed; non-apply always coerces to dry_run.

public_ruta_rutero_columns.fact: The new PUBLIC_INSERT_SQL inserts the same column list as the prior loader revision (cadena..modalidad,row_hash,source,source_row). Neither old nor new loader writes *_norm columns to public.ruta_rutero, so app/db.py (rr.cliente_norm) and sql/03 MV (rr.cliente_norm) behavior is unchanged by the column shape. The change is the write STRATEGY (full delete+insert per source vs ON CONFLICT upsert), not the column shape.
public_ruta_rutero_columns.evidence: git 074ee5b^ public INSERT column list identical to loader PUBLIC_INSERT_SQL; app/db.py:36 RUTA_TABLE; sql/03 line 30 rr.cliente_norm.

resolved_view_columns_vs_refresh.fact: scripts/refresh_control_gestion_v2_incremental.py reads f.cod_rt and f.cliente_norm from v_rr_frecuencia_base_resuelta_v2; sql/11 exposes both cod_rt and cliente_norm.
resolved_view_columns_vs_refresh.evidence: refresh WEEKLY_FREQ usage f.cod_rt (lines 381/616/645/733), cliente_norm; sql/11:232/237.

resolved_view_columns_vs_loader.fact: loader REQUIRED_RESOLVED_VIEW_COLUMNS and run_postcheck require cod_rt_norm, which sql/11's view does not expose (it exposes cod_rt). See blocker F-02.

canonical_builder.fact: scripts/cg_canonical_build_local.py already uses an explicit route_source_plan with week_start and source_ruta_batch_ids; it does not consume the new assignment table.

tracked_views.fact: sql/11 create-or-replace redefines cg_core.v_ruta_rutero_load_batch_week_v2 and v_rr_frecuencia_base_resuelta_v2 to depend on the new assignment table; the existing deployed views must be replaced for the feature, and that replacement carries F-01 (legacy backfill) and F-02 (cod_rt_norm).

removed_or_renamed_columns: resolved view: cod_rt_norm not exposed (loader/postcheck expect it) -> effectively a rename to cod_rt relative to loader expectations

findings.fact: The resolved view joins the union of explicit + legacy week-batch mappings; legacy_inferred excludes a batch only when THAT batch has an ACTIVE assignment, not when the week is explicitly assigned. A legacy batch whose loaded_at infers to the assigned week still contributes rows, so grains deleted in the explicit batch survive via legacy backfill.
findings.evidence: sql/11:104-126 legacy_inferred NOT EXISTS on a.ruta_batch_id=b.ruta_batch_id; resolved view joins v_ruta_rutero_load_batch_week_v2 (union) not v_ruta_rutero_latest_week_batch_v2.
findings.consequence: The 70 bajas for week 2026-06-08 persist; the replacement policy is silently defeated and postcheck blesses it.
findings.reproducible_scenario: Week 2026-06-08 assigned to new batch B; legacy batch 19 (loaded_at 2026-06-08T04:56Z -> Santiago Mon 2026-06-08) remains legacy-inferred to the same week; a grain present only in batch 19 appears in the resolved surface.
findings.required_correction: Resolve each week from the single winning batch (join v_ruta_rutero_latest_week_batch_v2) or exclude legacy_inferred for any week that already has an ACTIVE assignment; add a postcheck comparing resolved grains to the assigned batch.

findings.component: loader verify_db_contract / run_postcheck vs sql/11 resolved view
findings.fact: loader REQUIRED_RESOLVED_VIEW_COLUMNS and the duplicate-grain postcheck require cod_rt_norm, but the sql/11 resolved view exposes cod_rt (not cod_rt_norm).
findings.evidence: loader 144-157 (REQUIRED_RESOLVED_VIEW_COLUMNS includes cod_rt_norm), 1030-1043 (group by cod_rt_norm); sql/11:226-258 selects cod_rt.
findings.consequence: verify_db_contract returns missing_resolved_view_column:cod_rt_norm and aborts apply before any write; if bypassed, the duplicate-grain postcheck raises UndefinedColumn. The loader and its companion DDL cannot co-function.
findings.reproducible_scenario: Apply sql/11 views, then run --apply: verify_db_contract fails on cod_rt_norm.
findings.required_correction: Make the view expose cod_rt_norm, or change loader REQUIRED_RESOLVED_VIEW_COLUMNS and postcheck to cod_rt (matching refresh).

findings.fact: The apply never supersedes the prior ACTIVE assignment and hardcodes replaces_ruta_batch_id=None; a second (retroactive) replacement of the same week inserts a second ACTIVE row that violates the unique ACTIVE index.
findings.evidence: loader 1104-1111 create_week_assignment(..., replaces_ruta_batch_id=None); create_week_assignment INSERTs status 'ACTIVE' only; sql/11:80-82 unique partial index.
findings.consequence: The named retroactive-replacement cycle (B supersedes A; rollback reactivates A) is unimplemented; a same-week re-apply errors and rolls back. Only a first-ever load to a fresh week works.
findings.reproducible_scenario: Apply week 2026-06-08 (succeeds while table empty); apply a correction for the same week -> IntegrityError on ux_ruta_rutero_week_assignment_active -> rollback.
findings.required_correction: Within the transaction, lock and set the prior ACTIVE assignment for (week, policy) to SUPERSEDED before inserting the new ACTIVE, and set replaces_ruta_batch_id to the prior batch.

findings.fact: Postcommit rollback restoration is not implemented; the function validates arguments then unconditionally raises 'rollback_execution_requires_separate_authorization'. The SQL rollback is commented design only.
findings.evidence: loader 1154-1185; sql/11:261-279 comments; test_40 only checks callable.
findings.consequence: There is no executable way to restore public.ruta_rutero, reactivate the previous assignment, or mark the failed assignment ROLLED_BACK after a committed bad apply.
findings.reproducible_scenario: Call run_weekly_replacement_rollback with valid args -> raises before any restoration.
findings.required_correction: Implement and test the postcommit restoration transaction (or forbid apply until it exists).

findings.fact: POSTCHECK_CONTRACT lists 10 checks; run_postcheck implements 6, omits current_surface_hash_matches and no_stale_rows_from_previous_snapshot, and its duplicate-grain check references the non-existent cod_rt_norm.
findings.consequence: Named integrity guarantees (surface hash, no stale rows) are not enforced; a name in POSTCHECK_CONTRACT does not imply enforcement; legacy backfill (F-01) is not caught.
findings.reproducible_scenario: Inspect run_postcheck: no hash comparison, no stale-row query.
findings.required_correction: Implement the declared checks (including a stale/baja check) or remove the unimplemented names and reconcile column references.

findings.fact: The resolved_surface_hash written to the assignment (Python SHA-256 over source_row|row_hash of the current public surface) is never compared to any DB-computed hash; the only DB hash (fetch_current_snapshot_summary) is MD5 over row_hash alone (different algorithm and columns). The name is misleading (not derived from the resolved weekly view).
findings.evidence: loader current_surface_hash() vs fetch_current_snapshot_summary(); create_week_assignment writes resolved_surface_hash; previous_snapshot emitted but unused.
findings.consequence: No end-to-end hash equality check exists; the stored hash cannot be exactly compared to a post-insert DB recomputation.
findings.reproducible_scenario: Compare the two hash algorithms statically; no comparison code exists.
findings.required_correction: Define one canonical surface hash computable identically in Python and SQL, write it, and assert equality in postcheck.

findings.fact: The multirow tiebreak uses source_row asc; for grains tying on visitas and day-sum but differing in person, the selected route/person flips with Excel order/source_row, and the resolver collapses multirow groups while BD-CG005B-001 is still open.
findings.evidence: sql/11:211-220; isolated simulation: order1=REPO_A, order2=REPO_B; CG005B BD-CG005B-001 open.
findings.consequence: Non-deterministic route/person attribution across reloads for the person-only-differs class; pre-empts a pending Bastian decision.
findings.reproducible_scenario: Two rows, same visitas/day-sum, different reponedor; swap source_row order -> winner changes.
findings.required_correction: Add a business-meaningful, source_row-independent tiebreak (or block multirow collapse until BD-CG005B-001 is decided).

findings.fact: No advisory lock or SELECT ... FOR UPDATE on the week's assignments; concurrency is mediated only by row locks and the unique ACTIVE index.
findings.consequence: Two concurrent same-week applies can deadlock or fail opaquely; fails closed (no corruption).
findings.reproducible_scenario: Two operators run --apply for the same week concurrently.
findings.required_correction: Acquire a transaction-scoped advisory lock keyed on (week, policy) at the start of apply.

findings.fact: --skip-source-check is not rejected together with --apply; a skipped source-check returns 'warn', so apply proceeds without the source-check contract.
findings.evidence: loader build_arg_parser; source_check_payload_skipped; validate_cli_args (no skip+apply guard).
findings.consequence: Defense-in-depth is weakened; missing critical columns are caught only later inside transform (fails closed).

findings.fact: The week is validated only as ISO + Monday; it is not cross-checked against the workbook content or the contracted 2026-06-08..2026-06-14.
findings.consequence: An operator can assign any Monday to the load.
findings.reproducible_scenario: --apply with a different Monday than intended.
findings.required_correction: Optionally validate the week against an expected/contracted week or workbook evidence.

findings.fact: Explicit cur.execute('BEGIN') is issued after autocommit=False and after verify_db_contract has already started the transaction, producing a redundant nested-BEGIN warning.
findings.reproducible_scenario: Run apply against a real DB; observe the nested BEGIN warning.
findings.required_correction: Remove the explicit BEGIN or move all reads after it.

quality_assessment.contract_adherence: All nine validation sections (A-I) completed; integrity commands executed; findings matrix with the required fields; no productive files modified; no DB/Docker/SQL/apply; only the two authorized artifacts created.
quality_assessment.test_strength: Tests pass and guard the dry-run/CLI/dedup contract well, but they mock the DB and assert on SQL-file substrings, so they do NOT exercise the resolver semantics (F-01), the cod_rt_norm contract (F-02), the lifecycle supersede (F-03), or rollback restoration (F-04). Green tests therefore do not establish apply-readiness.
quality_assessment.semantic_correctness: BLOCKED: legacy backfill defeats the replacement policy; multirow tiebreak is source_row-dependent.
quality_assessment.transaction_safety: Atomic and fails closed, but unlocked and self-inconsistent with the view contract.
quality_assessment.compatibility: public.ruta_rutero column shape preserved; refresh/canonical compatible; loader vs resolved-view column contract is INCOMPATIBLE (cod_rt_norm).
quality_assessment.confidence_basis: Integrity verified by executed commands; semantic findings derived by static reading of self-consistent artifacts and an isolated ordering simulation. The DB-dependent claims (F-01, F-02) are proven by reading the committed SQL/loader but were not run against a live database (no DB access authorized).
unresolved_items: Whether the currently-deployed v_rr_frecuencia_base_resuelta_v2 exposes cod_rt_norm (would change whether F-02 manifests at verify time vs view-replace time); requires read-only DB inspection in an authorized phase.
Exact count of the 31 non-exact multirow groups that tie on visitas AND day-sum (the order-dependent subset); requires the workbook/DB.
Whether public.ruta_rutero.cliente_norm/cod_rt_norm are generated columns (assumed yes from identical old/new insert lists); confirm against the live table DDL.
